title: $C^0$ Hamiltonian dynamics and a counterexample to the Arnold conjecture
speaker: Sobhan Seyfaddini
abstract: After introducing Hamiltonian homeomorphisms and recalling some of their properties, I will focus on fixed point theory for this class of homeomorphisms. The main goal of this talk is to present the outlines of a $C^0$ counterexample to the Arnold conjecture in dimensions four and higher. This is joint work with Lev Buhovsky and Vincent Humiliere.

proof_stages.label: Classical Arnold conjecture as target
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by recalling Arnold’s fixed-point lower bound for Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms on a closed symplectic manifold and announcing that the analogous statement will fail for Hamiltonian homeomorphisms in dimension at least four.

proof_stages.label: C0 symplectic and Hamiltonian categories
proof_stages.summary: Symplectic homeomorphisms are defined as C0 limits of symplectic diffeomorphisms, and Hamiltonian homeomorphisms are then defined via Müller–Oh continuous Hamiltonian flows, requiring both convergence of flows and C0 convergence of the generating Hamiltonians.

proof_stages.label: Sanity checks suggesting a C0 fixed-point theory
proof_stages.summary: Uniqueness of continuous Hamiltonians, preservation of coisotropic characteristic foliations, and the surface Arnold theorem are presented as evidence that continuous Hamiltonian dynamics retains enough smooth Hamiltonian structure that an Arnold-type theorem seemed plausible.

proof_stages.label: Sharp counterexample theorem and surviving invariants
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is made precise: in every closed symplectic manifold of dimension at least four there is a Hamiltonian homeomorphism with exactly one fixed point, smooth symplectic off that point, and the failure persists for any normal-subgroup candidate definition, while spectral-invariant statements may survive in a modified form.

proof_stages.label: Invariant tree carrying all fixed points
proof_stages.summary: The hard construction is to build a Hamiltonian homeomorphism psi and an invariant tree T such that every fixed point of psi lies on T, psi is smooth symplectic off T, and the tree is assembled edge-by-edge using a quantitative h-principle and four-dimensional general-position room.

proof_stages.label: Collapse-conjugation to one fixed point
proof_stages.summary: A sequence of symplectic diffeomorphisms is chosen to converge C0 to a map collapsing the invariant tree T to a point p and symplectic off T; conjugating psi by these maps yields a limiting homeomorphism with no fixed points away from p and with p as its unique fixed point.

proof_stages.label: Hamiltonian-control upgrade
proof_stages.summary: The construction must be refined, or checked at every step, so that the approximating Hamiltonians also converge in the Müller–Oh sense, thereby upgrading the one-fixed-point limit from the closure of Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms to an actual Hamiltonian homeomorphism.
